item.title: Time’s running out to get a free gift card when you preorder a new MacBook
item.description:
Source: The Verge
This has been a week to remember here at The Verge. MWC 2026 in Barcelona lead straight into Apple’s week of product announcements. There was truly something for everyone, whether you’ve been wanting a company to make a phone that can start fires, or had your fingers crossed for a brand-new MacBook model that costs […]
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3), launch(+2), who(+2)

item.title: Grammarly is using our identities without permission
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Grammarly's "expert review" feature offers to give users writing advice "inspired by" subject matter experts, including recently deceased professors, as Wired reported on Wednesday. When I tried the feature out myself, I found some experts that came as a surprise for a different reason - one of them was my boss. The AI-generated feedback included […]
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3), launch(+2), who(+2)

item.title: OpenAI robotics lead Caitlin Kalinowski quits in response to Pentagon deal
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Hardware executive Caitlin Kalinowski announced today that in response to OpenAI's controversial agreement with the Department of Defense, she’s resigned from her role leading the company's robotics team.
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Vizio accounts are becoming Walmart accounts
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Walmart bought Vizio in 2024, and now it's taking the next steps to formally fold the TV maker into itself by phasing out Vizio accounts. Now, when you purchase a new Vizio TV, you'll be asked to sign in or create a Walmart account. Customers will have the option to merge their Vizio account, if […]
Matched: ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: The OpenClaw superfan meetup serves optimism and lobster
item.description:
Source: The Verge
The woman at the door wore a plush lobster headdress. She sat in the front hallway of a multistory event venue in Manhattan, beside a bundle of wristbands. If she granted you one, the world of ClawCon beckoned behind her - full of vibey pink and purple lighting, lobster claw headbands, multicolored name tags, sponsor […]
Matched: law(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Apple’s cheap laptop looks like a winner
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 118, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, please take my Switch away so I can get some work done, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) This week, I've been reading about moon […]
Matched: breaking(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Valve says it still plans to ship the Steam Machine in 2026
item.description:
Source: The Verge
A blog post from Valve on Friday initially seemed to throw cold water on the idea that the Steam Machine, Steam Frame, and Steam Controller would arrive in 2026 at all. But Valve tells The Verge it did not mean to suggest that - and that all three pieces of hardware will indeed ship this […]
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Robinhood’s startup fund stumbles in NYSE debut
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The fund currently offers retail investors exposure to eight startups, including Mercor, Ramp, and Stripe, with plans to expand its portfolio.
Matched: ai(+3), startup(+2)

item.title: Nintendo sues the US government for a refund on tariffs
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
This lawsuit comes after a Supreme Court decision struck down some of the president's sweeping tariffs, which had impacted Nintendo and thousands of other companies.
Matched: law(+3), court(+2)

item.title: OSHA probing fatality at Rivian warehouse
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
A 61-year-old worker died on Thursday after reportedly getting stuck between a tractor trailer and a loading dock.
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic Claude remains available to non-defense customers
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: TriZetto confirms 3.4M people’s health and personal data was stolen during breach
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Health tech giant TriZetto has confirmed that more than 3.4 million people had personal and health information stolen in a 2024 cyberattack, which the company failed to detect for almost a year.
Matched: ai(+3), breach(+3)

item.title: OpenAI delays ChatGPT’s ‘adult mode’ again
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The feature, which will give verified adult users access to erotica and other adult content, had already been delayed from December.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Anthropic’s Pentagon deal is a cautionary tale for startups chasing federal contracts
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The Pentagon has officially designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk after the two failed to agree on how much control the military should have over its AI models, including its use in autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance. As Anthropic’s $200 million contract fell apart, the DoD turned to OpenAI instead, which accepted and then watched ChatGPT uninstalls surge 295%. As the stakes keep rising, the question remains: how much unrestricted […]
Matched: ai(+3), startup(+2)

item.title: A bite-sized adventure that puts a wrench into the classic Zelda formula
item.description:
Source: The Verge
There are a lot of games that try to emulate The Legend of Zelda, but few that manage to capture that spirit in such a small, concise package as Ratcheteer DX. The postapocalyptic game only takes a few hours to complete, but over that span it nails the classic Zelda vibe almost perfectly, offering a […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: DJI will pay $30K to the man who accidentally hacked 7,000 Romo robovacs
item.description:
Source: The Verge
On Valentine's Day, I brought you a story that's since made headlines all around the world: How one man, just trying to steer his DJI robot vacuum with a PlayStation gamepad, discovered an entire network of 7,000 remote-control DJI robots ready to let him peek into other people's homes. To be clear, DJI had already […]
Matched: who(+2)

item.title: Claude’s consumer growth surge continues after Pentagon deal debacle
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Claude's app is now seeing more new installs than ChatGPT and is growing its daily active users.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: City Detect, which uses AI to help cities stay safe and clean, raises $13M Series A
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
City Detect, a company that helps local governments prevent urban decay, is in at least 17 cities so far, including Dallas and Miami.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Quantum scale-up Pasqal plans $2B SPAC listing, promises to ‘remain French’
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
A SPAC deal that is set to take French quantum company Pasqal public on the Nasdaq values the company at $2 billion pre-money.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: India’s Karnataka signals intent to ban social media for under-16s
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Karnataka's proposal highlights a growing global debate over children’s rights and online policy.
Matched: policy(+3)

item.title: PopSockets founder David Barnett talks about building a viral business
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
David Barnett, founder of PopSocket, stops by Equity to talk about scaling the business and what he's learned as a leader.

item.title: Sony appears to be testing dynamic pricing on PlayStation games
item.description:
Source: The Verge
A site called PSprices has been tracking prices on Sony's digital game store and noticed something unusual: some games were being offered at different prices to different users. What's more, those offers are tracked in the PlayStation API with experiment identifiers like IPT_PILOT and IPT_OPR_TESTING. Dynamic pricing is nothing new and is used across a […]

item.title: The Corvette ZR1X hybrid can outpace million-dollar sports cars for a fraction of the cost
item.description:
Source: The Verge
When Elon Musk burst onto the scene in his little Tesla Roadster, it seemed a matter of time before electricity rendered gas-powered sports cars obsolete. It hasn't worked out that way. Automakers have struggled to bring purely electric two-seaters to market. The ones that managed to emerge have been flatly rejected by consumers. Porsche has […]
